original: Romulus and Remus found Rome (traditional date)
Otto I the Great grants Utrecht fishing rights
Dedication of the transept and choir of Abbey of Ste Madeleine Vezelay, France, a masterpiece of Romanesque architecture and an important place of pilgrimage
The Santiago de Compostela Cathedral to St James is consecrated before King Alfonso IX of Leon (begun 1075)
France and England sign an anti-Spanish military agreement
Maryland Toleration Act passed in the American colony, allowing freedom of worship for Christians but sentencing to death anyone who rejected the divinity of Jesus
Battle of Pułtusk: Swedish army defeats a Saxon-led force during the Great Northern War
Spain and Naples-Austria sign a peace treaty
NYC formally declares coast of Ellis Island publicly owned, so they can build forts to protect NYC from British
Franz Grillparzer's "Sappho" premieres in Vienna
Battle of San Jacinto, Texas wins independence from Mexico
1st train crosses Mississippi River's 1st bridge, from Rock Island, Illinois to Davenport, Iowa
Alexander Douglas patents the bustle
Congress establishes US Mint in Denver, Colorodo
Ellen Price Wood's "East Lynne" premieres in Boston
Bahá'u'lláh, founder of the Bahá'í Faith, enters the garden of Ridván near Baghdad. He makes his declaration as a Messenger of God during the 12 days he spends there.
First Lady Lucy Hayes begins egg rolling contest on White House lawn
New York installs its first fireman's pole
Ship Azor leaves Charleston with 206 blacks for Liberia
Potters Field reopened as Madison Park
Black longshoremen strike for higher wages in St Louis, Missouri
Phillies pitcher Bill Duggleby hits a grand slam in his first at-bat
The elective assembly of Crete proclaims union with Greece
American polar explorer Frederick Cook claims to have been the 1st person reach North Pole on this date (disputed)
Cleveland Naps play 1st game at League Park, lose to Detroit Tigers 5-0
German passenger ship Imperator runs aground
US marines occupy Vera Cruz, a major Mexican port; they will stay for 6 months
The Aud, carrying a cargo of 20,000 rifles to assist Irish republicans in staging what would become the 1916 Rising, is captured by the British Navy and forced to sail towards Cork Harbour
Ulster Protestant and Irish nationalist Sir Roger Casement lands at Tralee Bay, Ireland from a German submarine; discovered at McKenna's Fort and arrested by the Royal Irish Constabulary
John Galsworthy's "Skin Game" premieres in London
The first Aggie Muster is held as a remembrance for fellow Aggies who had died in the previous year
Chuvash Autonomous Region in RSFSR becomes Chuvash ASSR
Fire at Ohio State Penitentiary kills 322
Senators catcher Moe Berg, plays AL record 117th consecutive errorless game
King Boris of Bulgaria forbids all political parties
1st $64 Question, "Take It or Leave It," on CBS Radio
WWII: Greece surrenders to Nazi Germany
NFL Chic Cardinals & Pittsburgh Steelers merge (dissolves on Dec 3)
Ivor Novello's "Perchance to Dream" premieres in London
WWII: Allied forces conquer Ie Shima, Okinawa in 5 days; 5,000 die
WWII: Allied troops occupy German nuclear laboratory in Haigerloch
WWII: Soviet army arrives at outskirts of Berlin, Germany
WWII: US 7th Army reaches Nuremberg, Germany
Socialistic Einheitspartei Germany forms in East Germany
Stanley Cup Final, Maple Leaf Gardens, Toronto, ON: Toronto Maple Leafs beat Montreal Canadiens, 3-2 for a 4-1 series victory
BOAC begins 1st passenger service with jets (London-Rome route)
Secretaries Day is first celebrated, known today as Administrative Professionals Day
USAF flies French battalion to Vietnam
Brooklyn Dodgers win, then record 10th straight game to begin a season
Minas Gerais, Argentina, tunnel caves in; 30 die
Alf Dean, using a rod and reel, hooks a 2,664 lb, 16' 10" great white shark off the coast of Ceduna, Australia
Brasilia becomes capital of Brazil
Founding of the Orthodox Bahá'í Faith in Washington, D.C.
Dirk Stikker chosen as secretary general of NATO
French army revolts in Algeria
USAF Maj Robert M White takes X-15 to 32,000 m
Century 21 Exposition opens in Seattle, Washington
The Universal House of Justice of the Bahá'í Faith is elected for the first time
Pirates & Cubs combine for 9 HRs, Pirates win 8-5
New York World's Fair reopens for 2nd & final season
Dodgers 1st rain out in Los Angeles (after 737 consecutive games)
Evangelical Broadcasting begins in Netherlands
Joseph Stalin's daughter Svetlana Allilueva arrives in New York City after defecting to the US
Regime of the Colonels: Military junta overthrows Greek government in a coup, and Konstantinos Kollias becomes premier
22nd Tony Awards: "Rosencranz and Guildenstern Are Dead" (play) & "Hallelujah, Baby!" (musical) win
The Ministry of Defence in London announces that British troops would be used in Northern Ireland to guard key public installations following a series of bombings
Reds clout 7 HRs by 6 batters, Braves counter with 3 HRs, 6 for one team & 9 different batters for two teams, all tie or set records
The Alliance Party of Northern Ireland (APNI) is formed; it attempts to appeal to Catholics and Protestant to unite in support of moderate policies
The Principality of Hutt River (previously Hutt River Province) secedes from Australia - it remains unrecognised by Australia or other nations
Original Codex Reguis (with Edda-liederen) returns to Iceland
John Young & Charles Duke explore the moon as part of NASA mission Apollo 16
Orbiting Astronomical Observatory 4 (Copernicus) launched
28th Tony Awards: "The River Niger" (play) & "Raisin" (musical) win
Swine Flu vaccine, for non-epidemic, enters testing
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Krystyna Chojnowska-Liskiewicz of Poland is the first woman to sail single-handed around the world, arriving back in the Canary Islands after 401 days
Howard Stern begins broadcasting on WWWW Detroit, Michigan
Soviet submarine catches fire off the coast of Okinawa, Japan, killing at least nine
US furnishes $1 billion in arms to Saudi Arabia
Atlanta Braves win their 13th straight game
Dutch Queen Beatrice addresses US Congress
£1 coin introduced in the United Kingdom
"Nightline" reverts from 1 hour to ½ hour format
Centers for Disease Cont says virus discovered in France causes AIDS
Franz Weber of Austria skis downhill at a record 209.8 kph
Montreal Expo David Palmer no-hits St Louis Cards, 4-0 in a perfect 5 inn game
Bomb attacks in NATO/AEG-Telefunken building in Brussels
Bob Hering sets Formula One power boat record (165.338 mph, Ariz)
Dow Jones Industrial Average soars 664.7; 2nd biggest one-day gain in history
MLB Milwaukee Brewers lose for the first time of the season, ending a 13-game winning streak start
Tamil bomb attack in Colombo Sri Lanka, 115 killed
Thousands of Chinese crowd into Beijing's Tiananmen Square, cheering for students demanding greater political freedom
"Cartoon All Stars to Rescue" shown on all 4 TV networks
MLB Cincinnati Reds win, running their record to 9-0, best start in club history
National League umpire is arrested for stealing baseball cards
French underwater archaeologist Franck Goddio discovers the San Diego, Dutch galleon sunk in 1600 off Fortune Island in the Philippines
Greatest extra-inning comeback, Pitts scores 6 in bottom of 11th erasing 5 run Cub lead, Pirates also trailed 7-2 in bottom of 9th
Jakov Tolstikov wins 4th World Cup marathon (2:09:17)
NFL Draft: University of Miami defensive tackle Russell Maryland first pick by Dallas Cowboys
Mobil Oil tug with 12,000 gallons of oil run aground in Arthur Kill (a tidal strait separating Staten Island, New York City from mainland New Jersey, USA)
Robert Alton Harris is put to death in the California Gas Chamber for 3 murders
Brazil votes against a monarchy
Serbian army bombs distress clinic in Goradze Bosnia, 28 killed
The first discoveries of extrasolar planets are announced by astronomer Alexander Wolszczan
"Kiss of Death", directed by Barbet Schroeder and starring Nicholas Cage and David Caruso, premieres in America
Boston Celtics final regular season game at Boston Garden, NY Knicks win 98-92
British sitcom "Father Ted" written by Arthur Mathews & Graham Linehan, starring Dermot Morgan premieres on Channel 4
"Delicate Balance" opens at Plymouth Theater NYC
Chicago Bulls win NBA record 72 games (72-8)
Matabeleland beat Mashonaland Country Dist to win Logan Cup
Wayne James scores 99 & 99 & ct 11 stp 2 in Logan Cup Final
I. K. Gujral sworn in as Prime Minister of India
NFL Draft: Virginia Tech quarterback Michael Vick first pick by Atlanta Falcons
The United States Air Force retires the F-117 Nighthawk stealth surface-attack aircraft
Chicago White Sox pitcher, Philip Humber, pitches the 21st MLB perfect game against the Seattle Mariners
Two trains in Sloterdijk, Netherlands, injure 117 people in a head on collision
185 people are killed in a conflict between Islamic extremists and the Nigerian military
Horacio Cartes is elected President of Paraguay
Tsegaye Kebede and Priscah Jeptoo win the 2013 London Marathon
Taliban attack army base at Mazar-e-Sharif, Afghanistan, killing more than 100
Oakland A's left hander Sean Manaea no-hits the Boston Red Sox
Terror attacks on churches and hotels on Easter Sunday in three Sri Lankan cities, Colombo, Negombo and Batticaloa kill at least 253 and injure hundreds
At least 25,000 extra people across 11 countries have died during COVID-19 pandemic that were not previously counted according to new mortality figures

Health Secretary Matt Hancock and UK government come under criticism for lack of personal protective equipment and testing for COVID-19 (despite pledge to test 100,000 a day only 19,000 done April 20th)
Mexico says it is entering stage three of its COVID-19 outbreak, with rapid spread and hospitalizations from 8,772 infections and 712 deaths
Indonesian submarine RI Nanggala sinks off the coast of Bali with the loss of all 53 people on board
Manhattan district attorney's office announces it will no longer prosecute prostitution, dismissing 914 open cases, part of growing movement to change approach to prostitution
Ex-president of Honduras Juan Orlando Hernández extradited to the US to face drug trafficking and weapons charges
US Supreme Court rules abortion pill mifepristone can remain widely available, blocking ban by Texas U.S. District Judge
Chevron Championship Women's Golf, The Club at Carlton Woods: In beating Maja Stark by 2 strokes, Nelly Korda wins her second major title and equals LPGA record with 5 consecutive wins
Battle of Mutina:Mark Antonyis again defeated in battle by Consul Aulus Hirtius, who is killed in the fighting, forcing Antony to lift his siege. DecimusBrutusis murdered shortly after.
Sentencia Arbitral de Guadalupe (Arbitration Sentence of Guadalupe) proclaimed by Spanish KingFerdinand II of Aragon, freeing Catalan remensa peasants
KingHenry VIII's chief ministerThomas Cromwellbegins to plotAnne Boleyn's downfall while feigning illness
First date inJames Clavell's novel Shogun
Catherine IIof Russia confers on the nobility the "Charter to the Nobility", increasing further the power of the landed oligarchs
John Adamssworn in as 1st US Vice President (9 days before Washington)
Brazilian revolutionaryTiradentesis hanged, drawn, and quartered in Rio de Janeiro
Joséphine de Beauharnais, future Empress of French and first wife of Napoleon Bonaparte, is imprisoned during the Reign of Terror
Napoleonand the French defeat the Piedmontese at Battle of Mondovi
Danish scientistHans Christian Ørstedis the first to identify electromagnetism, when he observes a compass needle
Abraham Lincoln's funeral train leaves Washington
PopeLeo XIIIpublishes encyclical Inscrutabili
George Bernard Shaw's play "Arms and the Man" premieres in London becoming his first public success
Ty Cobbmakes his professional debut for the Augusta Tourists in the South Atlantic League
German World War I fighter ace Baron Manfred vonRichthofenaka "The Red Baron," is shot down and killed over Vaux-sur Somme in France. Canadian pilot Arthur Roy Brown is credited with the kill.
No baseball games played in NL due toCharles Ebbets' funeral
Noël Coward's controversial comedy play "Fallen Angels" premieres in London, after initially being refused a license due to the loose morals of the female characters
"The Passion ofJoan of Arc", directed by Carl Theodor Dreyer, starring Renée Jeanne Falconetti and Eugène Silvain, is released in Denmark

"All Quiet on the Western Front" based on the novel byErich Maria Remarque, directed by Lewis Milestone, starring Louis Wolheim and Lew Ayres premieres in Los Angeles (Academy Awards Outstanding Production 1930)
German philosopherMartin Heideggerelected rector of University of Freiburg
GeorgiMalenkovbecomes premier of USSR
Elvis Presley's first hit record, "Heartbreak Hotel," reaches #1
PopePius XIIpublishes encyclical Fidei Donum
Beatles meetthe Rolling Stonesfor the first time
Ethiopian EmperorHaile Selassievisits Kingston, Jamaica
"Julie and Dick In Covent Garden", music and comedy special starringJulie AndrewsandDick Van Dyke, withCarl Reiner, premieres on ABC-TV
Last South Vietnam PresidentNguyễn Văn Thiệuresigns after 10 years in power
Billy Martinpulls Yankee line-up out of a hat, beats Blue Jays 8-6
Ziaur Rahmanappointed president of Bangladesh
After 37 weeksMichael Jackson's album "Thriller" is knocked off as top album by movie soundtrack for "Footloose"
Ingrid Kristiansenof Norway wins London Marathon in race record 2:21:06
Geraldo RiveraopensAl Capone's vault on live TV and finds nothing, except great ratings for his spectacle
Barbra Streisandrecords "You'll Never Know"

World premiere of sports fantasy film "Field of Dreams", in Dubuque, Iowa;  based on W. P. Kinsella's novel "Shoeless Joe", and starringKevin Costner,James Earl Jones,Ray LiottaandBurt Lancaster

Action TV series "Walker, Texas Ranger" starringChuck Norrisdebuts on CBS
Eddie Murraysets record for switch hit HRs in a games (11 times)
FBI arrestsTimothy McVeighin the Oklahoma City bombing case; He  is indicted on 160 state offenses and 11 federal offenses, including the use of a weapon of mass destruction
Ashes of psychologist and author Timothy Leary and "Star Trek" creatorGene Roddenberrylaunched into space orbit
Rock for the Rainforest benefit concert held at Carnegie Hall, NYC; performers include:Sting,Elton John,James Taylor,Billy Joel,Bette Midler, and Little Jimmy Scott
Brandon Sanderson's debut novel "Elantris" is published (winner of Romantic Times award for best epic fantasy of 2005)
US PresidentBarack Obamabegins a 4 day visit to the UK with MichelleObama

Ukrainian comedian VolodymyrZelenskyywins the country's presidential election in a landslide
South African PresidentCyril Ramaphosaannounces $26 billion aid package and plans for gradual reopening of economy as cases reach 3,465 with 58 deaths

Pope Francis, head of the Catholic Church since 2013, dies; succeeded by Pope Leo XIV
First Battle of Panipat: Central Asian conquerorBaburdefeats Sultan Ibrahim Lodi, establishing the Mughal Empire in India
Spanish–American War: The U.S. Congress, on April 25, recognizes that a state of war exists between the United States and Spain
